text.title: Business Storytelling Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding business-storytelling. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The artefact MUST lead with the central claim within the first 2 sentences. Burying the answer past sentence 3 is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Executives stop reading the moment their question is answered or visibly delayed.

rule.statement: Supporting arguments MUST be MECE (Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive). Overlapping or non-exhaustive lists are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Overlap signals sloppy thinking; gaps signal missing analysis. Both erode credibility.

rule.statement: Every fact MUST be paired with its implication. Bare facts ('revenue grew 20%') are forbidden — must add 'which means...'.
rule.rationale: A fact without an implication is incomplete; the audience must infer the point themselves, which is the slow path.

rule.statement: Change narratives (pivot, layoffs, strategy shift) MUST use SCQA or Pixar — Pyramid alone reads as a directive without context.

rationale.source: Dan & Chip Heath — Made to Stick

rule.rationale: Without narrative tension, change stories trigger defense; SCQA / Pixar arc earns the buy-in.

rule.statement: An artefact MUST use exactly one framework — Pyramid OR SCQA OR Pixar. Mixing produces incoherent arcs.
rule.rationale: Mixing frameworks reads as draft scaffolding the author forgot to remove.
